prose:
Computed condensed Fukui functions for pyridine using chemkit’s fukui-reactivity skill at DFT level (functional B3LYP, basis def2-TZVP; tier `standard`) with exact four-center integrals (no RI), in the gas phase.

Method provenance
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (nucleophilic attack), f⁻ (electrophilic attack), dual descriptor f².
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, no density fitting).
• Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by user (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz”); single-point charges for N, N±1 electron states calculated at identical coordinates.
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion states treated as doublets (charges +1/−1, multiplicity 2) by default.
• Software/backend: PySCF 2.x via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill.
• Convergence & integrity: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true. No engine warnings reported. Integrity checks flagged as informational warnings:
  – "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
  – "common header present"
  All checks passed (ok=true) and do not indicate issues.

Key results (atoms numbered as in the supplied .xyz):
• Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): Atom 1 (N), f⁺ = 0.48422572
• Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻): Atom 4 (C), f⁻ = 0.11826260

Headline numeric result reported above is the maximum f⁺ value.

Live log: the run was streamed to a file during execution (path given by chemkit: “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/argo_o3/20260706-191710_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260706-191710.out”, available for real-time inspection).

Provenance: computed (this work).
